meaning: every minute

A.note: Perfectly natural; capitalization irrelevant per instructions.

A.note: Perfectly natural and common in schedules/notes.

meaning: every day at noon

A.note: Perfect, standard way to express this.

B.note: Too brief; lacks the 'every day' frequency.

C.note: Natural, though slightly more colloquial than A.

D.note: Natural and concise.

meaning: every Monday through Friday at 9:30 a.m.

A.note: Perfectly natural and grammatically correct for a mid-sentence fragment; lowercase 'montags' is correct here.

B.note: Correct, but the capitalization of 'Montags' is unnecessary for a mid-sentence fragment.

C.note: Correct, but the capitalization of 'Montags' is unnecessary for a mid-sentence fragment.

D.note: Sounds like a telegram or a list; less natural for embedding mid-sentence than A.

meaning: every day at 9 a.m. and 5 p.m.

slate.text: Jeden Tag um 9 Uhr und um 17 Uhr.

A.note: Correct and natural, though slightly repetitive with the double 'um'.

B.note: Very concise; common in digital contexts, but lacks the word for 'daily'.

C.note: Standard professional phrasing for schedules.

D.note: Most natural for spoken or informal written German.

meaning: every hour from 9 a.m. through 5 p.m.

A.note: Perfectly natural and accurate for a mid-sentence fragment.

B.note: Correct, but capitalization and punctuation are unnecessary for a fragment.

C.note: Correct, but capitalization and punctuation are unnecessary for a fragment.

D.note: Grammatically correct but sounds slightly more clunky/formal than 'stündlich'.

meaning: every minute from 0 through 29 past the hour

slate.text: in den Minuten 0 bis 29 jeder Stunde
Jede Minute von Minute Null bis Minute 29 jeder Stunde.
Jede Minute von Minute 0 bis 29 jeder Stunde.

A.note: Natural and concise, though slightly less explicit about the 'every single minute' aspect.

B.note: Too elliptical; it describes a range but not the frequency/recurrence.

C.note: Redundant ('Minute' mentioned thrice) and writing out 'Null' is unconventional for schedules.

D.note: Accurate but slightly repetitive/clunky due to the double use of 'Minute'.

meaning: on the 1st and 15th at midnight

slate.text: Um 00:00, an Tag 1 und 15 des Monats

A.note: Perfectly natural; 'jeweils' clarifies that midnight occurs on both dates.

B.note: Very natural, slightly different emphasis than A.

C.note: Too technical and clunky; sounds like a literal translation of a database field.

D.note: Grammatically correct and common, though slightly less precise than A/B regarding the repetition.

meaning: on January 1 at noon

slate.text: Um 12:00, an Tag 1 des Monats, nur im Januar

A.note: Incorrect meaning; implies any January 1st, but only during January, and is phrased unnaturally.

B.note: Natural and accurate, though 'mittags' is slightly less precise than '12 Uhr'.

C.note: Perfect, standard German for this meaning.

D.note: Perfect, standard German for this meaning.

meaning: every day at midnight

A.note: Accurate, but less descriptive than 'midnight'

B.note: Perfectly natural and precise

C.note: Perfectly natural; identical to B in quality

D.note: Perfectly natural and precise

meaning: every 30 seconds

A.note: Perfectly natural and accurate.

B.note: Correct, but contains a period which violates the 'sentence fragment' requirement.

C.note: Perfectly natural and accurate.

D.note: Perfectly natural and accurate; the lowercase start is ideal for mid-sentence embedding.

meaning: on the last Friday of the month at midnight

slate.text: am letzten Freitag im Monat um Mitternacht
am letzten Freitag des Monats um Mitternacht
am letzten Freitag des Monats um Mitternacht
Um 00:00, am letzten Freitag des Monats

A.note: Very natural, common in spoken and written German.

B.note: Perfectly accurate and the most standard phrasing.

C.note: Identical to B; perfectly accurate.

D.note: Slightly clunky word order for a mid-sentence fragment; '00:00' is more technical than 'Mitternacht'.

meaning: at five and ten seconds past the minute, every Monday at 9:30 a.m.

slate.text: Jeden Montag um 09:30 Uhr und 10 Sekunden.
Jeden Montag um 9:30 Uhr und 5 Sekunden.
Bei Sekunde 5 und 10, bei Minute 30, um 09:00, nur jeden Montag
in den Sekunden 5 und 10 jeder Minute, um 9:30 Uhr montags

A.note: Incorrect; suggests one single point in time (9:30 and 10s) rather than two distinct seconds.

B.note: Incorrect; only mentions 5 seconds, misses the 10-second mark.

C.note: Accurate but reads like a technical list or a manual, not a natural sentence fragment.

D.note: Most natural phrasing that accurately captures both the specific seconds and the specific time/day.

meaning: every 15 seconds, at 30 minutes past the hour from 9 a.m. through 5:30 p.m. on Monday through Friday

slate.text: Montags bis freitags von 09:00 bis 17:30 Uhr alle 15 Sekunden ab der halben Stunde jeder Stunde.
Montags bis freitags alle 15 Sekunden ab halb nach jeder Stunde von 9:00 bis 17:30 Uhr.
Alle 15 Sekunden, bei Minute 30, zwischen 09:00 und 17:59, Montag bis Freitag
alle 15 Sekunden, in Minute 30 jeder Stunde, von 9 bis 17:30 Uhr montags bis freitags

A.note: Grammatically correct, but 'ab der halben Stunde jeder Stunde' is repetitive and slightly clunky.

B.note: 'halb nach jeder Stunde' is incorrect; 'halb' always refers to the half-hour mark of the current hour, not an offset from the start.

C.note: The time range 17:59 contradicts the required 17:30 limit.

D.note: Clear and concise. 'in Minute 30' is a common way to describe a specific timestamp in technical schedules.

meaning: every Monday at 9:30:15 a.m.

A.note: Perfect, most natural way to express a recurring time in German.

B.note: Clunky and sounds like a manual override or a specific restriction.

C.note: Correct and natural, though slightly more emphatic than 'montags'.

D.note: Correct; the leading zero in the hour is common in digital contexts but less natural in flowing text.

meaning: every day at 12:09:45 a.m. and 12:17:45 a.m.

slate.text: Jeden Tag um 00:09:45 Uhr und 00:17:45 Uhr.
Bei Sekunde 45, bei Minute 9 und 17, um 00:00

A.note: Standard, precise, and natural phrasing.

B.note: Incorrect and unnatural; reads like a literal list of parameters rather than a schedule.

C.note: Natural and correct, though slightly less formal than using leading zeros for midnight.

D.note: Standard, precise, and natural phrasing.

meaning: every minute from 0 through 30 past the hour, at 9 a.m., 5 p.m., 6 p.m., and 7 p.m.

slate.text: in den Minuten 0 bis 30, um 9, 17, 18 und 19 Uhr
Jede Minute von Minute 0 bis 30 um 9:00, 17:00, 18:00 und 19:00 Uhr.
Jede Minute von Minute 0 bis 30 um 9:00, 17:00, 18:00 und 19:00 Uhr.

A.note: Concise and idiomatic for a schedule description.

B.note: Incorrect time range (17:00 to 19:59 includes 20:00 minus one minute, and the phrasing is clunky).

C.note: Redundant ('Jede Minute von Minute 0'), though accurate.

D.note: Identical to C; redundant phrasing.

meaning: every day at 12 p.m., 10 p.m., 11 p.m., 12 a.m., 1 a.m., and 2 a.m.

slate.text: Täglich um 12 Uhr mittags sowie von 22 bis 2 Uhr nachts.

A.note: Standard, precise, and professional.

B.note: Natural, though 'von 22 bis 2 Uhr' implies a continuous range rather than specific hourly ticks.

C.note: Grammatically incorrect and confusing.

D.note: Correct, but slightly too colloquial/clipped for most contexts.

meaning: every hour from 9 a.m. through 8 p.m. and at 10 p.m.

slate.text: Stündlich von 9 bis 20 Uhr sowie um 22 Uhr.
Stündlich von 9 bis 20 Uhr und dann noch einmal um 22 Uhr.
stündlich von 9 bis 20 Uhr und um 22 Uhr

A.note: Perfectly natural and precise professional phrasing.

B.note: Grammatically incorrect; 'um' cannot be used with a time range 'bis'.

C.note: Natural, though slightly more colloquial than A.

D.note: Accurate and natural; essentially a more concise version of A.

meaning: every minute during the 9 a.m., 12 p.m., and 5 p.m. hours on Monday through Friday

slate.text: Jede Minute, um 09:00, 12:00, und 17:00, Montag bis Freitag
jede Minute von 9 bis 9:59 Uhr, von 12 bis 12:59 Uhr und von 17 bis 17:59 Uhr montags bis freitags
Montags bis freitags jeweils jede Minute in der 9., 12. und 17. Stunde.
Montags bis freitags jede Minute in den Stunden von 9, 12 und 17 Uhr.

A.note: Incorrectly implies three specific moments in time rather than three whole hours.

B.note: Very precise and unambiguous, though slightly wordy.

C.note: Using 'Stunde' as an ordinal (9. Stunde) refers to the 9th hour of the day, which is logically correct but unnatural in this context.

D.note: Grammatically correct and clear, though slightly more formal/stilted than B.

meaning: on the weekday nearest the 15th in June at 9:30 a.m.

slate.text: Am Werktag, der dem 15. Juni am nächsten liegt, um 9:30 Uhr.
Um 09:30, am Werktag am nächsten zum 15 Tag des Monats, nur im Juni
am Werktag, der dem 15. Juni am nächsten liegt, um 9:30 Uhr.
am nächsten Werktag zum 15. im Juni um 9:30 Uhr

A.note: Grammatically correct and precise, though slightly formal.

B.note: Awkward phrasing ('15 Tag') and inaccurate logic ('nur im Juni').

C.note: Identical to A; natural and accurate.

D.note: Ambiguous; 'am nächsten Werktag zum 15.' sounds like the first working day following the 15th, not necessarily the nearest.

meaning: on February 29 at midnight

slate.text: Um 00:00, an Tag 29 des Monats, nur im Februar

A.note: Perfectly natural and accurate.

B.note: Perfectly natural and accurate.

D.note: Overly literal, unnatural, and grammatically clumsy.

meaning: every minute from 1 through 59 past the hour

slate.text: Jede Minute von der ersten bis zur 59. Minute nach der vollen Stunde.
Jede Minute von Minute 1 bis 59 nach der vollen Stunde.
in den Minuten 1 bis 59 jeder Stunde

A.note: Grammatically correct, but slightly redundant (Minute... Minute).

B.note: Phrasing is imprecise and sounds like a technical setting rather than a natural description.

C.note: Clear and natural, though 'nach der vollen Stunde' is slightly formal.

D.note: Most concise and natural way to express this in German.

meaning: one minute and one second past the hour, every hour

slate.text: Jede Stunde eine Minute und eine Sekunde nach voll.
Jede Stunde zur ersten Minute und ersten Sekunde.
in Minute 1 und Sekunde 1 jeder Stunde

A.note: Very natural and idiomatic use of 'nach voll' to denote past the hour.

B.note: Sounds like technical parameters or coordinates rather than a time description.

C.note: Grammatically correct, but 'zur ersten Minute' is slightly formal/stiff.

D.note: Sounds like a literal translation of a technical log; not how a native speaker describes a schedule.

meaning: on December 31 or on Friday in December at 11:59 p.m.

slate.text: Am 31. Dezember oder an einem Freitag im Dezember um 23:59 Uhr.
am 31. Dezember oder an einem Freitag im Dezember um 23:59 Uhr.
Um 23:59, an Tag 31 des Monats, und jeden Freitag, nur im Dezember
am 31. oder freitags im Dezember um 23:59 Uhr

A.note: Grammatically correct; slightly formal due to capitalization, but fits the meaning perfectly.

B.note: Perfectly natural and accurate as a mid-sentence fragment.

C.note: Very unnatural phrasing ('Tag 31') and changes the logic to 'and' instead of 'or'.

D.note: Clear, but 'am 31.' is slightly colloquial; it assumes the month from the later part of the sentence.

meaning: every second

A.note: Correct, but often implies 'every second' as an adjective/adverb meaning 'per second' or 'constantly'.

B.note: Same as A; natural but less precise for a timed interval than 'jede Sekunde'.

C.note: Perfectly natural and accurate. Capitalization ignored per instructions.

D.note: Perfectly natural and accurate; matches the requested fragment style.

meaning: five seconds past the minute, every minute

slate.text: Alle sechzig Sekunden, immer fünf Sekunden nach der vollen Minute.
Alle sechzig Sekunden, jeweils fünf Sekunden nach jeder vollen Minute.

A.note: Grammatically okay, but slightly clunky; usually 'in der 5. Sekunde' or 'bei Sekunde 5'.

B.note: Natural and concise, though it implies the 'every minute' part through context rather than explicitly stating it.

C.note: Accurate, but a bit repetitive and wordy for a sentence fragment.

D.note: Very precise and grammatically sound, though slightly formal.

meaning: five minutes past the hour, every hour

B.note: Too vague; it lacks the 'every hour' frequency.

C.note: Colloquial but grammatically incorrect; 'voll' cannot be used this way.

D.note: Idiomatic, concise, and precisely matches the English meaning.

B.note: Means 'at five past ten' (10:05).

C.note: Accurate but slightly wordy for a mid-sentence fragment.

D.note: Commonly used in scheduling contexts to denote specific minute marks.

Alle zwei Stunden jeweils jede Minute von Minute 0 bis 30.
